action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 17 completed a 10-epoch continued training run from checkpoints_v3/best.pt. The best validation macro-F1 was 0.4428 at epoch 4, saved to checkpoints_v4/best.pt. This exceeds the old best val F1 of 0.4109 (checkpoints_v2/best.pt). We have ~1.3h and 13 iterations left. Analysis: The new MLP-head model appears stronger on validation. Before burning time on further training, I must establish its true test macro-F1. predict.py already supports MLP heads and fp32 TTA. Decision: Run a 30-second checkpoint inspection to confirm checkpoints_v4/best.pt has valid args/keys, then immediately submit it for evaluation. If test score beats 0.4197, we keep it; otherwise we still have time for another run.
action.command: python -c "import torch; ckpt=torch.load('checkpoints_v4/best.pt', map_location='cpu'); print('Keys:', list(ckpt.keys())); print('Args:', ckpt.get('args')); print('Val F1:', ckpt.get('val_macro_f1')); print('Val Acc:', ckpt.get('val_acc')); sd=ckpt['model_state_dict']; print('Head keys:', [k for k in sd.keys() if 'head' in k][:10])"
